stories.title: German lawmaker downplays Trump troop withdrawal
stories.summary: A lawmaker from German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s party said Tuesday he is not worried about U.S. plans to pull troops from his country — and he suggested those forces may be shifted to Poland or Romania. “We are not concerned at all about the 5,000 troops because they will not be withdrawn,” said Roderich Kiesewetter, a lawmaker from the Christian Democratic Union, during the POLITICO Security Summit. “They will be posted as a sign of deterrence and resolve far more eastern in Europe. Be it to

stories.title: Six people found dead inside train car at rail yard near Texas-Mexico border
stories.summary: Discovery was made by Union Pacific employee inspecting stopped train at the yard in Laredo before it continued its journey north Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Rail workers in Texas found six people dead inside a boxcar at a yard close to the Mexican border on Sunday afternoon, officials said. The discovery was made by a Union Pacific employee inspecting the stopped train at the yard in Laredo before it continued its journey north, a spokesperson for the Laredo police departm

stories.title: French film industry at risk from the far right, say actors and directors
stories.summary: Juliette Binoche joins 600 leading figures to warn against a ‘fascist takeover of the collective imagination’ More than 600 cinema figures have said the growing influence of the far right on French cinema production risks turning into a “fascist takeover of the collective imagination”. In an open letter published in the newspaper Libération to coincide with the opening of the Cannes film festival , they said the billionaire Vincent Bolloré ’s dominant position in French film production and distr

stories.title: US Democrats urge Jen Kiggans to resign for agreeing with racist ‘cotton-picking’ remark
stories.summary: The Republican congresswoman says she did not condone radio host’s language US politics live – latest updates Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Jen Kiggans, a Republican congresswoman, is facing calls from Democrats to resign for agreeing with a radio host after he said the top US House Democrat, Hakeem Jeffries , should get his “cotton-picking hands off of Virginia”. Kiggans, who represents a swing district in southeastern Virginia, has said she was agreeing with the host that J

stories.title: Marty Makary departs FDA after clashes with Trump over fruit-flavored vapes
stories.summary: Kyle Diamantas, a top official at the agency, will replace Makary and serve as acting FDA commissioner Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Marty Makary resigned from his position as commissioner of the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on Tuesday, concluding a 13-month tenure at the regulatory agency that frequently drew the ire of the White House, Congress, industry and the public, Donald Trump confirmed on Tuesday. Kyle Diamantas, who previously worked as the top food officia

stories.title: Incoming Hungarian justice minister promises to re-establish rule of law and crack down on corruption
stories.summary: Márta Görög said she intends to work strictly according to professional standards, free from political interests and ideologies, when speaking before the Committee on Judicial and Constitutional Affairs.

stories.title: Vitézy: Sok százmilliárd forint ment el egy olyan álomra, amiről soha senki nem kérdezte meg a magyar embereket
stories.summary: A leendő miniszter „luxusszállodákat megszégyenítő” nagyzolást látott Rogán Antal egykori minisztériumában.

stories.title: Rubovszky Rita a katolikus püspöki konferencia oktatásügyi főtanácsadója lett
stories.summary: Rubovszky feladata az lesz, hogy az egyház nevében tárgyaljon az állami szervekkel az oktatás-nevelést érintő témakörökben.

stories.title: Karikó Katalint a tejfölös pult mellett érte Hegedűs Zsolt tanácsadói felkérése
stories.summary: A leendő miniszternek azt mondta, vállalja, de csak ingyen, és csak akkor, ha nem a nevének szólt a felkérés.

stories.title: A GKI szerint pont a kiemelt állami támogatást kapó ágazatok tudták legkevésbé növelni a termelékenységüket
stories.summary: Míg az elmúlt 15 évben több ágazatban is fejlődött a magyar gazdaság, ez nem bizonyult elegendőnek ahhoz, hogy széles körű felzárkózás történjen az EU-hoz.

stories.title: Magyar Péter felszólítására hét távozó miniszter közölte, hogy hoztak olyan döntést, amivel pénzügyi kötelezettséget vállaltak
stories.summary: Az új miniszterelnök hétfő estig kért beszámolót az Orbán-kormány tagjaitól.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás nélkül készültek a közös fotók a Sándor-palotában a miniszterek kinevezési okmányainak átadásán
stories.summary: Sulyok Tamás hivatalosan is kinevezte a tizenhat minisztert, akik később az Országgyűlés előtt tesznek esküt. Némi zavar után Sulyok Tamás végül nem állt oda a közös fotókhoz, az okmányok átadása után Magyar Péter pózolt a kinevezett miniszterekkel. A Magyar-kormány éjféltől lép hivatalba.

stories.title: Felesküdött miniszterek, Magyar Péter legrövidebb beszéde és egy sajátos fotózás Sulyoknál – félnapos élő a kormányalakításról
stories.summary: Magyar Péter kormányának tagjai esküt tettek a parlamentben, ezzel hivatalosan is elkezdi a munkát a következő kabinet. Élőben kapcsoltuk a parlamentet, a stúdióban pedig a Telex újságírói elemezték az elmúlt napok legfontosabb történéseit.

stories.title: Magyar és olasz munkahelyek bánják, hogy a háztartási gépeknél is jönnek fel a kínaiak
stories.summary: Túl drága az európai gyártás, és már innovációval is nehéz megverni a kínai importot, az Electrolux a magyar kivonulás után Olaszországban is zsugorodik.

stories.title: Meghalt Gulyás Zsolt Bács-Kiskun megyei rendőrfőkapitány
stories.summary: Az 54 éves Gulyás Zsolt munkavégzés közben rosszul lett, kórházba szállították, de az orvosi ellátás ellenére meghalt.

stories.title: A bíróság megsemmisítette a TEK főigazgatójának jogsértő határozatát a volt telexes újságírók ügyében, újra kell vizsgálni a panaszukat
stories.summary: Halász Júliától és Sarkadi Zsolttól a kamerát is megpróbálták elvenni a rendőrök Szijjártó Péter kalocsai fórumán 2024-ben.

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid rekreálta Orbánék négy évvel ezelőtti kormányszelfijét a miniszterjáraton
stories.summary: Új kormány, új kisbusz, új csoportkép.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely és Rétvári Bence is gratulált az új minisztereknek
stories.summary: Megalakult Magyar Péter első kormánya, a miniszterek délután már átvették a kinevezésüket Sulyok Tamástól. Éjféltől hivatalosan is vége az Orbán-kormánynak, Magyarék szerdán már kormányülést tartanak.

stories.title: A Belügyminisztérium sikerpropagandával reagált a Tisza karmelitás idegenvezetésére
stories.summary: Miután Magyar Péter bemutatta, mekkora pénzszórás ment a Karmelitában, Pintér Sándor minisztériuma az épületben vezetett sétákkal kezdett el felvágni.

stories.title: Magyar government officially formed, he says the task ahead is enormous
stories.summary: Péter Magyar said they will work to put the country back on track after two decades of neglect. The new cabinet will hold its first formal meeting on Wednesday.

stories.title: Tizenhat miniszterrel megalakult Magyar Péter kormánya
stories.summary: Az Országgyűlés ünnepi ülésen letették az esküt a Tisza-kormány miniszterei. Magyar Péter szerint az új kormányban négy tárcának kiemelt szerepe lesz a rendszerváltásban.

stories.title: Felvidék–what's behind the term that has once again put Slovak-Hungarian relations on edge?
stories.summary: Péter Magyar clashed with the Slovak government over the Beneš Decrees while still in opposition, but it was his use of the term “Felvidék” that drew the most criticism. Lawsuits have since been filed over this issue. Experts believe that Slovaks and Hungarians should finally understand each other’s grievances, and that politicians should stop fuelling tensions.

stories.title: Az euró vágya nagyon jókor jön, most lehet végrehajtani egy ilyen nagy fordulatot
stories.summary: Hogyan lenne érdemes áthangolni a gazdaságpolitika fókuszát a növekedés beindításához? Miért lenne célszerű bevezetni az eurót? A Magyarország jövő időben új adása Szabó Balázzsal, a Hold vezérigazgatójával és az Egyensúly Intézet szakértőivel.

stories.title: A közbeszéd mindig a startupokról és a kkv-król szól, de nem ők fogják meghozni a gazdasági növekedést
stories.summary: A világon mindenhol a közepes és a nagyvállalatok adják a húzóerőt, nekünk is rájuk kell építenünk. A Magyarország jövő időben legújabb részében arra kerestük a választ, hogyan lehetne felgyorsítani a gazdaság növekedését.

stories.title: Magyar Péter videón mutatta be a Belügyminisztérium milliárdokért felújított épületét
stories.summary: Amikor megtekintette Rétvári Bence parlamenti államtitkárságát, Magyar azt mondta, hogy olyan illat van, mint egy luxusszállodában.

stories.title: Profi focista lett Amerikában a Ted Lasso legkedvesebb szereplője
stories.summary: A Dani Rojast alakító színész az amerikai másodosztályba igazolt.

stories.title: EU approves sanctions on Israeli settlers after Hungarian backing
stories.summary: The sanctions — which include asset freezes and travel bans and will also target Hamas members — come amid growing international criticism of Israel’s settlement expansion and escalating violence in the occupied territories.

stories.title: Hungary’s next health minister Zsolt Hegedűs dances at Magyar celebration
stories.summary: Large crowds gathered outside the Hungarian parliament building to celebrate the official inauguration of Peter Magyar as prime minister. Attendees included incoming Health Minister Zsolt Hegedus, who went viral for his dancing on the night of the country's April election.

stories.title: "We've lived with them for 80 years": Inside a German town that US troops could soon leave after Donald Trump's withdrawal threat
stories.summary: If US President Donald Trump is serious, 5,000 US soldiers could be withdrawn from Germany. This would include the Stryker Brigade in Vilseck. The town is in shock.

stories.title: Hungary: New govt intent on initiating swift systemic change
stories.summary: Peter Magyar and his government are determined to push ahead with change at full throttle. But even before the cabinet is sworn in, there has been a "resignation." And questions remain about Magyar's Ukraine policy.

stories.title: Israeli MPs back special tribunal with death penalty powers for alleged 7 October attackers
stories.summary: Knesset approves plan for livestreamed trials in military court, drawing comparisons to 1962 Adolf Eichmann trial Israeli lawmakers have approved setting up a livestreamed special tribunal with the power to sentence to death Palestinians convicted of taking part in the Hamas attack on 7 October 2023 that triggered the war in Gaza. The measure was passed by 93 votes to none in the 120-seat Knesset, Israel’s parliament, reflecting widespread support among Israel’s Jewish majority for punishing tho

stories.title: Kyiv’s corruption scandal threatens Zelenskyy’s EU push
stories.summary: The timing couldn’t be worse for Volodymyr Zelenskyy.  Just as the Ukrainian president is pushing to secure rapid EU membership for Ukraine, a deepening corruption scandal in Kyiv is undermining his case that the country is ready to join.  On Monday, prosecutors charged Andriy Yermak — once one of Zelenskyy’s closest confidantes — with corruption and money laundering.  In a statement, Ukraine’s National Anti-Corruption Bureau (NABU) and the Specialized Anti-Corruption Prosecutor’s

stories.title: Pahlavi criticizes Trump for sending mixed signals on Iran
stories.summary: President Donald Trump should stop sending mixed messages about its intentions in the Iran war and instead “finish the job” of ousting Tehran’s Islamist regime, the son of Iran’s last shah said Tuesday. Reza Pahlavi’s criticism of Trump comes as the U.S. and Iran have exchanged proposals for a long-term peace process amid a fragile ceasefire. Pahlavi, who in general has backed Trump, said the U.S. and Israel should continue airstrikes and other attacks on Iranian targets until Iranian citizens f

stories.title: Chaos in Philippines as Duterte ally wanted by ICC takes refuge in senate to avoid arrest
stories.summary: Former police chief Ronald dela Rosa spends night at senate office after another Duterte ally offers protective custody The unusual pursuit was captured on CCTV cameras inside the Philippine senate. Ronald dela Rosa, a longtime ally of the former president Rodrigo Duterte, raced along the hallways of the upper house complex, stumbling on the staircase, as he fled government agents. “They want to forcibly bring me to The Hague, to surrender me there,” Dela Rosa said later on a Facebook livestream

stories.title: Head of Microsoft’s Israel branch to step down after inquiry into dealings with Israeli military
stories.summary: The inquiry came after the Guardian revealed Israel used company technology to support mass surveillance of Palestinian phone calls The head of Microsoft ’s Israeli subsidiary will step down in the wake of an inquiry that has scrutinised its business dealings with the Israeli military. Microsoft ordered the inquiry last year in response to a Guardian investigation revealing the military had used the company’s technology to operate a powerful surveillance system that collected Palestinian civilia

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia breaks ceasefire overnight, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy has said that Moscow launched some 200 drone strikes in a single night, bringing an end to a temporary truce. Moscow meanwhile said it intercepted 27 Ukrainian drones overnight.

stories.title: Nigeria airstrike kills 100 at market, Amnesty says
stories.summary: Amnesty International is calling for an investigation, saying the strike was the latest deadly attack to kill civilians. The military denies that civilians were harmed.

stories.title: South Africa's top court bars repeat asylum applications
stories.summary: The home affairs minister hails the ruling as a "victory" against the "abuse" of the refugee system.

stories.title: Germany’s Merz booed at major trade union congress
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz faced a hostile reception at a major trade union congress in Berlin on Tuesday, with delegates repeatedly heckling and booing him as he defended painful economic reforms. Addressing the congress, Merz argued Germany could no longer preserve prosperity without overhauling its economy and social system. “We have simply failed to modernize our country,” the Christian Democrat leader said. “Germany must therefore pull itself together.” The mood in the hall quickly so

stories.title: Trump’s Beijing trip should make Taiwan ‘nervous,’ Slotkin says
stories.summary: Taiwan should be “nervous” about President Donald Trump’s trip this week to Beijing, a prominent Senate Armed Services Committee member said Tuesday, as surging gas prices and an uncertain U.S. ceasefire with Iran threaten to give China greater leverage. The president’s eagerness to strike a deal — “any deal” — with Chinese leader Xi Jinping risks overturning decades of delicately managed diplomacy on Taiwan, Sen. Elissa Slotkin, a Michigan Democrat and former Pentagon official warned at PO

stories.title: UK pledges drone boats to help secure Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: LONDON — The U.K. has pledged on Tuesday to provide autonomous vessels to safeguard the Strait of Hormuz in the event of a stable ceasefire in the Middle East. Defence Secretary John Healey offered the new British-built uncrewed surface vessels (USVs) to a U.K.-French-led multinational mission to reassure international shipping in the event of a lasting truce. The promise came as Healey chaired a meeting of defense ministers in London against a backdrop of continued U.K. political turmoil. Keir

stories.title: Pentagon’s estimate for Iran war grows to $29B
stories.summary: The cost of the Iran war has risen to $29 billion, a top Pentagon official told lawmakers Tuesday, even as the U.S. says combat operations have largely ceased. The estimate, provided by acting Pentagon Comptroller Jules Hurst at budget hearings in the House and Senate Appropriations panels, increased from the $25 billion the administration  told lawmakers last month  and is still significantly lower than outside projections. The latest figure comes amid a shaky ceasefire, competing nav

stories.title: Sicily police investigate illegal race with horse-drawn carts and Kalashnikovs
stories.summary: Horses seized and several people questioned after animal rights activist shares video of race along country roads A video showing an illegal horse race in Sicily, with spectators firing pistols into the air and brandishing Kalashnikov rifles, has prompted a police investigation that has led to the seizure of the animals. stories.title: European ministers to discuss sending rejected asylum seekers to third-country hubs
stories.summary: Exclusive: Council of Europe to meet in Moldova on Friday, with human rights body expected to stress countries’ right to control borders Europe live – latest updates European ministers will this week discuss plans to send thousands of rejected asylum seekers to third-country hubs, the head of the continent’s human rights body has told the Guardian. Alain Berset, the secretary general of the Council of Europe, said discussions about the removal of people who arrived in Europe by irregular routes

The government has also set aside federal cash for its proposed nation

stories.title: Russian ship that sank near Spain may have been carrying nuclear reactors to North Korea
stories.summary: Western military may have targeted the Ursa Major, which went down after mysterious explosions, reports suggest A Russian cargo ship that suffered a series of mysterious explosions before eventually sinking off the south-east coast of Spain 17 months ago may have been carrying nuclear submarine reactors destined for North Korea, according to reports. The Ursa Major, a 142-metre-long, Russian-flagged ship owned by the state-linked Oboronlogistics company, was purportedly sailing from St Petersbur

stories.title: Investor jitters over Starmer uncertainty drive UK borrowing costs to 28-year high
stories.summary: Bond yields soar and pound falls against dollar as investors brace for potential Labour leadership change UK politics live – latest updates Long-term UK borrowing costs soared to the highest level in almost three decades on Tuesday as fears about a change of Labour leadership triggered investor jitters and warnings of further bond market turmoil. With investors worried about potential changes to Labour’s tax and spending plans, the yield – in effect the interest rate – on 30-year government bond

stories.title: Starmer needs sharper survival strategy if he is to stay on
stories.summary: Tepid cabinet support and a blunted No 10 operation are making it harder for the prime minister to face down critics UK politics: live updates The last time Keir Starmer faced a threat to his leadership, his core team assembled in the cabinet room and persuaded ministers to fire off a succession of supportive tweets in an attempt to keep him in office. This time has been different. As the number of MPs calling for the prime minister to resign has grown over the last 48 hours, much of the cabinet

stories.title: Epstein survivors give tearful testimony in House field hearing in Palm Beach
stories.summary: Oversight panel Democrats hold event at ‘the scene of the crime’ in Florida and ask Trump to not pardon Maxwell Democrats tore into government’s handling of the Jeffrey Epstein abuse scandal on Tuesday – revealing new details of the scale of his international sex trafficking ring, and warning Donald Trump not to grant a presidential pardon to the late sex offender’s sidekick Ghislaine Maxwell. Several survivors of Epstein’s abuse also gave tearful testimony at a congressional field hearing in Fl

stories.title: Online safety campaigners reveal Starmer frustrations after Phillips exit
stories.summary: PM accused of dragging heels on forcing tech firms to block transmission of nude photos on children’s phones Internet safety and children’s rights campaigners say they have been frustrated for months by Keir Starmer’s lack of leadership on blocking child abuse images on children’s phones, speaking out after Jess Phillips resigned from the government saying she was tired of seeing “opportunities for progress stalled and delayed”. The influential Labour politician was one of four ministers who qui

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: US war on Iran has cost around $29bn, Pentagon says
stories.summary: Pete Hegseth and other officials appear before House to face grilling on Iran war expenditure and military operations Iran has expanded its definition of the strait of Hormuz into a “vast operational area” far wider than before the war, according to a senior officer in the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy in comments likely to anger the US. The strait is no longer viewed as a narrow stretch around a handful of islands but instead has been greatly enlarged in scope and military signi

stories.title: Starmer given a lifeline after Streeting challenge fails to materialise
stories.summary: Downing Street insiders suggest health secretary does not yet have the support for a leadership push UK politics live – latest updates Keir Starmer was increasingly confident that he had seen off the immediate threat to his job on Tuesday after a challenge from Wes Streeting failed to materialise despite several of the prime minister’s allies quitting the government. Downing Street insiders suggested that the health secretary did not yet have the required support from the 81 MPs he needed to for

stories.title: War in Iran: Despite Iranian attacks, Doha steps up mediation efforts
stories.summary: Qatar is intensifying regional mediation efforts despite the Iran war bringing airstrikes and cutting off Doha's access to global oil markets. But can Qatar leverage its ties to the US and Iran for its own benefit?

stories.title: EU invites Taiban members to discuss Afghan migrant returns
stories.summary: The European Commission has invited officials from Afghanitan's Taliban to discuss the returns of certain migrants. It's controversial on humanitarian grounds, and because Brussels doesn't recognize Taliban authority.

stories.title: Canvas owner secures student data in deal with hacking group
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters hacking group stole student data from the popular education platform Canvas last week. Parent company Instructure apologized for the hack and said Canvas "remains safe to use."

stories.title: Canvas hack: Company pays criminals to delete students' stolen data
stories.summary: The company behind Canvas says it has "reached an agreement" with the hackers who disrupted thousands of colleges and universities.

stories.title: A weakened Trump to test China's appetite for deal-making
stories.summary: A quagmire in Iran, sky-high oil prices and shrinking support at home: when US President Donald Trump belatedly travels to China this week, his position has weakened. Still, he is hoping for any deal. Nervous Europeans are watching.

stories.title: Tens of thousands march in Brussels during Belgium national strike
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of protesters marched through Brussels during a national strike called by Belgian unions against the government’s fiscal reforms.

stories.title: Hamas 'weaponised' sexual violence in 7 October attacks, Israeli investigation says
stories.summary: The most comprehensive report of its kind details harrowing cases of sexual attacks which it says were systematic.

stories.title: Dali ship operator charged over deadly Baltimore bridge collapse
stories.summary: Six construction workers were killed and several vehicles plunged into the river after the cargo ship crashed into the bridge.

stories.title: The Kabul rehab centre hit by deadly Pakistani strike
stories.summary: Pakistan says it hit 'military and terrorist infrastructure' - but the UN and victims' families reject this claim.

stories.title: King’s Speech: Palace tells No. 10 to keep Charles out of Starmer’s crisis
stories.summary: LONDON — Buckingham Palace privately asked whether King Charles III should proceed as planned with the ceremonial state opening of parliament on Wednesday, as Keir Starmer faces a mounting leadership crisis that threatens to force him out of office.  According to people familiar with the matter, granted anonymity to speak freely, the king’s team made clear in conversation with Starmer’s officials the importance of protecting the monarch from any impression that he is being used for politica

stories.title: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict at record high in 2025
stories.summary: The 32.3m surpasses those caused by disasters for the first time, as 82.2m people displaced in total around world The number of internal displacements triggered by conflict or violence around the world reached a record high in 2025, surpassing the number of disaster-driven internal displacements for the first time. stories.title: More than 110 Nobel laureates call on Iran to release gravely ill activist Narges Mohammadi
stories.summary: As human rights advocate is treated in Tehran hospital after transfer from Zanjan prison, prize winners demand her freedom More than 110 Nobel laureates have called for the immediate and unconditional release of Narges Mohammadi, the imprisoned Iranian human rights activist and Nobel peace prize laureate, after she was transferred to hospital amid concerns over her rapidly deteriorating health. stories.title: Irish TV to air Father Ted instead of Eurovision final in protest against Israel’s inclusion
stories.summary: National broadcaster RTÉ accused of antisemitism for decision to screen satirical 1996 Eurovision episode in boycott of contest It is considered one of the funniest episodes of a beloved sitcom, but the Father Ted storyline about Eurovision has been dragged into the row over Israel’s participation in this week’s song contest. stories.title: ‘Unprecedented’ global effort gives new name to polycystic ovary syndrome – and new hope to millions of women
stories.summary: Decades-long campaign powered by patient perspectives results in switch from PCOS – a name that caused confusion and undue suffering – to PMOS • What is PCOS, what are the symptoms and treatment, and why is it being renamed PMOS? • ‘I still want to scream’: the loneliness and confusion of living with PMOS After more than a decade of global consultation, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) – a condition that affects one in eight women – has been renamed. The hormonal disorder, estimated to impact 17

stories.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina left vulnerable by policy clash with US, representative says
stories.summary: Christian Schmidt, who is resigning post, says multi-ethnic nation may fall apart amid pressure from US and Russia The UN high representative for Bosnia and Herzegovina has warned about the possible destruction of the multi-ethnic state after he was forced to resign in a policy clash with the US, seemingly complicated by the commercial interests of a firm linked to Donald Trump Jr that is seeking to make investments in the region. stories.title: UAE’s secret attack on Iran risks drawing Gulf states into the war
stories.summary: If current precarious ceasefire between US and Iran ends, Emirates are more likely to be targeted by Tehran Middle East crisis: live updates The risk of some Gulf states becoming embroiled in a direct war with Iran has risen after it was reported the United Arab Emirates had secretly launched a major attack on Iran during the conflict. In addition, Kuwait has said that at least four members of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps had been captured trying to carry out “terrorist attacks” on the

stories.title: Auschwitz survivor who returned to live in Germany dies aged 101
stories.summary: Albrecht Weinberg spent years teaching students about Nazi atrocities after being imprisoned at Auschwitz and Belsen Three Auschwitz survivors tell their stories Albrecht Weinberg, who survived a series of Nazi concentration and death camps and lost most of his family in the Holocaust before returning to Germany in his 80s, has died. Weinberg died in Leer, north-western Germany, weeks after his 101st birthday and the premiere of a film about his life, Es Ist Immer in Meinem Kopf (It Is Always in

stories.title: WHO head tells countries to prepare for more hantavirus cases
stories.summary: Health officials in Paris say French woman who contracted disease on MV Hondius is on ventilator in intensive care The head of the World Health Organization has told countries to prepare for more hantavirus cases as authorities in Paris said a French woman who contracted the virus onboard the MV Hondius had the most severe form of the disease and had been put on a ventilator. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus thanked Spain for the “compassion and solidarity” it had shown by taking in the stricken cruis

stories.title: Kash Patel denies excessive drinking allegations as ‘total farce’ in Senate hearing
stories.summary: FBI director also dismisses allegations of unexplained absences as Democrats challenge him over Atlantic report US politics live – latest updates Embattled FBI director Kash Patel has denied under oath allegations of excessive drinking and unexplained absences on the job, dismissing them as “baseless” during a fiery congressional hearing. Patel sought to push back as Democrats challenged him over the “extremely alarming” reports, which ran in the Atlantic, which they argued would a mount to a “g

stories.summary: Patel and Chris Van Hollen, ranking member of the Senate appropriations subcommittee, trade accusations in heated budget hearing Sign up for the Breaking News US email The Pentagon revealed on 29 April that the US war on Iran had cost about $25bn for roughly two months of spending. Today, when asked if there are any updated costs associated with the war, Jules Hurst III , chief financial official for the Pentagon, said: “At the time of testimony … it was $25bn dollars. But the joint staff team a

stories.title: Streeting to meet Starmer on Wednesday as the prime minister defies calls to step down – UK politics live
stories.summary: A number of Streeting’s allies resigned from their ministerial posts on Tuesday and called for Keir Starmer to quit Starmer tells cabinet he will not quit without leadership challenge Here are some pictures from No 10 this morning. Darren Jones , the chief secretary to the PM, is now being interviewed on the Today programme. Nick Robinson , the presenter, is asking him if he knows whether Keir Starmer has decided how to respond to the pressure on him to resign. Jones is avoiding the question, as

stories.title: The big questions hanging over the Trump-Xi meeting in China
stories.summary: Taiwan, tariffs and the strait of Hormuz are on the meeting’s agenda for Beijing – but will the US president be forced to ask for help in ending his war with Iran? On 20 February, a White House official confirmed that US president Donald Trump would be travelling to Beijing the following month to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping. Top of the agenda: the US-China trade war. One week later, Trump approved joint strikes with Israel against Iran, starting a new war in the Middle East. Its ramifica

stories.title: What now for the role of high representative in Bosnia?
stories.summary: Christian Schmidt's departure as high representative in Bosnia and Herzegovina doesn't look like a routine diplomatic handover. It seems to indicate that international powers want something different from the role.

stories.title: India: New Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay cracks down on state-run TASMAC liquor shops
stories.summary: In his first major decision since becoming the chief minister of Tamil Nadu, Vijay has ordered 717 state-run TASMAC liquor stores near religious places and schools to shut down.

stories.title: Young hijab-wearing German beauty contestant takes on the AfD
stories.summary: As a Miss Germany contestant, Büsra Sayed wore a hijab on stage. This had never happened before — and the AfD didn't like it. Her response went viral.

stories.title: UK: Keir Starmer tells Cabinet he will continue in place, as four junior ministers quit
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has met with his Cabinet amid continued pressure after election losses. A set piece speech yesterday failed to calm the waters, and four junior ministers resigned on Tuesday.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz booed as he advocates economic reform
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz says Germany must buckle down to ensure economic renewal, amid geopolitical turmoil. Meanwhile, inflation is up again with energy prices a key driver pushing up costs. DW has the latest.

stories.title: 'Work not over,' says WHO after hantavirus evacuation
stories.summary: Extensive testing, contact tracing, and quarantine procedures are still needed to contain the outbreak, WHO says. However, it has stressed that the current outbreak is vastly different to the COVID-19 pandemic.

stories.title: Thailand scraps Cambodia naval pact amid sinking ties
stories.summary: Bangkok's decision to end MoU 44, a long-stalled maritime agreement with Cambodia, is a win for Thai nationalists. This comes as Cambodia struggles to garner international support amid controversy over scam centers.

stories.title: Trump-Xi summit: High stakes for the US, China and the world
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump is hoping for a foreign policy win at the upcoming summit with China's Xi Jinping. The Iran war, trade deals and Taiwan are expected to be high on the agenda during Trump's visit to Beijing.

stories.title: 2026 Rainbow Map delivers mixed picture on LGBTQ+ rights
stories.summary: ILGA Europe has released its annual ranking of LGBTQ+ rights across Europe and Central Asia, with Spain taking the top spot for the first time. But behind the numbers, activists warn of a growing backlash against LGBTQ+.

stories.title: As super 'El Nino' looms, wildfires are set to heat up
stories.summary: More than 150 million hectares — over twice the size of Texas — burned globally in the first months of 2026. With a high chance of a supercharged El Nino, the second half of the year could be even worse.

stories.title: Iran: Surge of political prisoners amid US-Israel war
stories.summary: Amid the US-Israel war with Iran, as the world breathlessly watches the Strait of Hormuz, Tehran is quietly increasing pressure on political prisoners. Advocates are sounding the alarm, warning of mass executions.

stories.title: China should stop hoarding food and fertiliser, says former World Bank chief
stories.summary: David Malpass also said that Beijing's claim to be a developing nation was no longer credible.

stories.title: Macron thanks Kipchoge for 'patience' after Nairobi morning run
stories.summary: Macron joined marathon legend Eliud Kipchoge for an early morning run in Nairobi on day three of his Africa tour, later thanking the two-time Olympic champion for his "patience."

stories.title: US passengers of hantavirus cruise ship being monitored in Nebraska
stories.summary: Two people are being monitored in Atlanta, 16 in Nebraska and another six passengers in Canada.

stories.title: Texas accuses Netflix of spying on users, including children
stories.summary: The lawsuit comes amid increased scrutiny over platform features like auto-play that deliver endless content to users.

stories.title: At least nine killed after bomb rigged to rickshaw explodes at north Pakistan bazaar
stories.summary: The Pakistani Taliban and other militant groups have grown more emboldened since the Afghan Taliban returned to power in Kabul in 2021.

stories.title: Mexico cancels plans to end school year early for World Cup
stories.summary: Parents were angry at a proposal to end classes on 5 June instead of 15 July due to the tournament and expected extreme heat.

stories.title: EU needs to delay social media access for children - von der Leyen
stories.summary: She told an EU summit that an expert panel was due to come up with steps on how to protect minors online by July.

stories.title: France seeks to move beyond colonial ties by meeting African leaders in Kenya
stories.summary: In a first since these meetings started in the 1970s, this move reflects a change in France's thinking.

stories.title: US in closely guarded talks to open new bases in Greenland
stories.summary: Negotiations have progressed in recent months, according to multiple officials familiar, with the White House optimistic of reaching a deal.

stories.title: Iran threatens weapons-grade uranium enrichment as peace talks falter
stories.summary: Tehran threatened to enrich uranium to weapons-grade if attacked again, as Trump said the ceasefire had a "one percent chance" of surviving after rejecting Tehran's proposal.

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran against weaponising Hormuz to 'blackmail' Gulf countries
stories.summary: Qatar warned Tehran on Tuesday against using the Strait of Hormuz as a "weapon" to hold Gulf states to ransom, as Doha and Ankara backed ongoing mediation efforts to end the Iran war and restore access to the vital waterway.

stories.title: eBay rejects $55.5bn offer from GameStop
stories.summary: The online auction giant said it doubted how the video game retailer would finance its offer.

stories.title: Chinese people divided ahead of Trump-Xi summit in Beijing
stories.summary: Residents in Beijing shared mixed opinions ahead of the upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with some hoping for improved ties and others expressing skepticism.

stories.title: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
stories.summary: Yoweri Museveni, 81, won disputed elections in January to extend his 40-year rule.

stories.title: Peacocks take over northern Italian beach town
stories.summary: More than 100 peacocks now roam Punta Marina in northern Italy, leaving residents searching for humane ways to manage the birds.

stories.title: Zelensky's ex-chief of staff in court as Ukraine corruption probe escalates
stories.summary: Andriy Yermak was named by Ukraine's two anti-corruption agencies as a suspect in a money-laundering scheme.

stories.title: Lebanon says two paramedics among 10 killed in Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Lebanon's health ministry says the paramedics were carrying out a rescue mission in Nabatieh in response to an earlier attack that killed one person.

stories.title: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
stories.summary: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.

stories.title: Kazakhstan launches driverless Light Rail Transit system after 10 years of delays
stories.summary: After a decade of construction delays, Astana has launched its Light Rail Transit (LRT) system, featuring driverless overground trains serving 18 stations across the city.

stories.title: US inflation jumps to 3.8% as energy costs surge from Iran war
stories.summary: The key measure of US inflation rises its highest level since May 2023 as consumers feel the impact of the Iran war.

stories.title: Palestinians gather to mark 78th anniversary of the Nakba and call for 'right of return'
stories.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and the Gaza Strip marked the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, a defining moment in Palestinian history dating back to 1948, when hundreds of thousands of people were forced to flee their homes, villages and land, becoming refugees.

stories.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
stories.summary: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

stories.title: Ex-Philippine leader Duterte's drug war enforcer escapes ICC arrest
stories.summary: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, a former national police chief, has sought refuge in the senate.

stories.title: Russia launches strikes at Ukraine, hours after expiration of 72-hour truce
stories.summary: On Sunday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine would retaliate if Russia decided "to return to a full-scale war."

stories.title: Pakistan struck a rehab centre and killed 269 Afghans. Their families want to know why
stories.summary: There are calls to investigate the 16 March airstrike - which the UN says likely killed more - as a war crime.

stories.title: Ukraine: EU sanctions Russians over 'systematic unlawful deportation' of children
stories.summary: The EU is sanctioning 16 individuals and seven entities over alleged child abductions. The UK also issued fresh sanctions, and Germany's defense minister visited Kyiv amid a shaky ceasefire. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Yes, Europe’s leaders are finally standing up to Trump. Here’s why | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz’s criticism of the US president was not a solo run. It was born of the realisation that US leverage has slipped Friedrich Merz’s criticism of Donald Trump last month reflected more than a moment of personal candour or a split between Berlin and the White House. It pointed to a broader shift under way among European leaders. Increasingly they are willing to publicly confront the Trump administration on issues ranging from Iran to Ukraine and European sovereignty. The Trump administ

stories.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging
stories.summary: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in ink Japan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade. Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Continue r

stories.title: Gaborone gold rush: how Botswana rose to the top of men’s sprinting
stories.summary: Country with a population of just 2.5m credits investment in young athletes for its rise but this progress is under threat It was a fairytale ending to the World Athletics Relays in Gaborone. In the final strait, Collen Kebinatshipi surged past South Africa’s Zakithi Nene to win the men’s 4x400m relay for Botswana. The home crowd, a sea of light blue, went wild. “It means so many things to us,” Letsile Tebogo, 22, the reigning 200m Olympic champion, who ran the second leg, told reporters afterwa

stories.title: Ukrainian children held in Russia: militarized, 'reeducated'
stories.summary: A Ukrainian initiative to repatriate abducted Ukrainian children reports that at least 20,000 girls and boys are being held by Russian families and authorities. Can these children ever find their way home?

stories.title: US: LA area mayor to plead guilty to acting as Chinese agent
stories.summary: The mayor of a Los Angeles suburb with a heavy Chinese-American presence has resigned after admitting to acting as a Chinese propaganda agent. Eileen Wang now faces a maximum penalty of 10 years in jail.

stories.title: EU negotiators agree new rules on vital medicines
stories.summary: A new agreement aims to tackle the drug shortages that have plagued the bloc. Among other things, it would make it easier to use public funds to support the production of essential medicines.

stories.title: South African president says he will not step down after impeachment call
stories.summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa said he would not resign on Monday evening after days of speculation.

stories.title: I led hikers up an Indonesian volcano - and then it erupted
stories.summary: An Indonesian guide who led hikers up Mount Dukono has described the moment of Friday's eruption which killed three people.

stories.title: Why welfare isn't winning elections in India like it used to
stories.summary: India's welfare politics still matters, but voters increasingly want jobs, growth and dignity too.

stories.title: How the Trump-Xi summit could set superpower relations for many years to come
stories.summary: Trump's whirlwind visit to China is the most significant for years - but will it bear fruit?

stories.title: Israel passes law to allow death penalty and public trials for those linked to 7 October
stories.summary: Israel passes a law that permits the death penalty and public trials for those involved in the unprecedented Hamas-led attacks.

stories.title: Iranian chief negotiator says US must accept peace proposal or face 'failure'
stories.summary: Iran has refused to back down in its war with Washington, with military officials warning they are prepared to respond to any renewed US attack.

stories.title: German ultrarunner Arda Saatçi completes 600-kilometre Death Valley to Los Angeles challenge
stories.summary: German athlete Arda Saatçi completed a 600-kilometre ultramarathon challenge from Death Valley to Los Angeles after running for more than 123 hours.

stories.title: Snack giant switches to black and white packaging as Iran war hits ink supplies
stories.summary: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely disrupted global supplies of energy and petrochemicals.

stories.title: Final passengers disembark hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
stories.summary: The final passengers aboard the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius disembarked in Tenerife before boarding flights to quarantine facilities in more than 20 countries.

stories.summary: A szövetségi kapitány a finnek és a kazahok elleni meccseken azt is szeretné látni, mire képes a csapat Szoboszlai Dominik nélkül.

stories.summary: Kedden folytatódott a miniszterjelöltek bizottsági meghallgatása a Parlamentben. Ma délután már esküt is tesznek a miniszterek, így éjféltől feláll az új kormány. A legfontosabb ígéreteket szedtük össze.

stories.summary: Közel 400 ezren voltak kénytelenek lemondani a lehetőségről 2022-ben, ugyanez a kör lehet most jogosult, de eltérő játékszabályokkal.

stories.summary: A 444 szakértője szerint Rogán Antal minisztériumába már csak az elég gyenge másodvonal jutott.

stories.summary: A köztársasági elnököt a képarány alapján levághatták a fotóról, ami azután készült, hogy Bóna Szabolcs átvette a miniszteri kinevezési okmányát a Sándor-palotában.

stories.summary: Már nem a szigetet akarják megszerezni, hanem csak növelni a katonai jelenlétet az amerikaiak.

stories.summary: Dmitrij Peszkov szerint azonnal véget érhet a háború, ha Zelenszkij meghozza a „szükséges döntést”.

stories.summary: A VIII. kerületi önkormányzat szerint a javaslat nem a rövid távú lakáskiadás teljes tiltására épül, hanem annak korlátozására és átláthatóbbá tételére.

stories.summary: Ideje felismernünk, hogy a sükösdi tambura lüktetése a saját identitásunk szerves része. Ez a zene kíméletlenül szembeállít a közös múltunkkal, a szegénységgel és az évszázados elhallgatásokkal. Cserébe zsigeri katarzist nyújt.

stories.title: Ruff Bálint: Tuzson úr, most facebookozik vagy beszélgetünk?
stories.summary: Ruff Bálint, a parlamenti meghallgatásán részletesen beszélt a terveiről, meg arról, mit látott Pintér Sándor és Rogán Antal volt irodáiban. Közben többször összeszólalkozott Tuzson Bencével.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás azért megoldotta, hogy legyenek közös képei a miniszterekkel

stories.title: Europe should behave more like China does if it wants to survive this age of chaos
stories.summary: Instead of obsessing over rules that have ceased to matter, we must consider giving Beijing a dose of its own medicine The US and Israel may have started the war in Iran, but – apart from the belligerents themselves – it is China and Europe that stand to lose the most from it. Yet while European leaders watch like rabbits caught in the headlights as energy prices shoot through the roof, China has responded to the crisis with remarkable equanimity. It is striking how self-confident Beijing is ahe

stories.title: Chicago teen who pushed for parents’ release from ICE custody dies of cancer
stories.summary: Kevin González, 18-year-old who had terminal colon cancer, died shortly after reuniting with his parents in Mexico A Chicago -born teen who advocated for his parents’ release from US immigration authorities’ custody while fighting terminal cancer has died shortly after reuniting with them in Mexico, his family has told media outlets. The parents of 18-year-old Kevin González had been taken into Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) custody in Arizona in mid-April after they crossed the US

stories.title: EU preparing to offer key concession to UK in new post-Brexit agricultural deal
stories.summary: Exclusive: Britain expected to be allowed to keep ban on live animal exports, sources say, in fillip for Keir Starmer UK politics live – latest updates Brussels is preparing to offer Keir Starmer a key concession in talks over an agricultural deal, giving the beleaguered prime minister an important victory in his efforts to move closer to the EU. European officials have conceded that the UK can keep its ban on live animal exports as part of any joint deal on food and agricultural products, accor

stories.title: Germany news: 1-in-6 retailers fear for future
stories.summary: About one-in-12 German businesses worry about survival, with twice as many retailers jittery. Meanwhile in Stuttgart, five pro-Palestinian activists face trial over attacking an Israeli defense company site. Follow DW.

stories.title: Oil jumps 4% as Trump rejects Iran’s response to ceasefire proposal
stories.summary: Oil prices jumped on Monday morning while European markets edged lower and Asian stocks rose to new all time highs after US President Donald Trump rejected Tehran’s response to the latest proposal on ending the war in Iran.

stories.title: French farmers dump manure at TotalEnergies refinery in fuel price protest
stories.summary: French farmers have staged a protest outside a major oil refinery near Lyon, dumping manure and rubbish at the entrance in anger over rising fuel costs and falling incomes.

stories.title: Sudan drone strikes killed at least 880 civilians between January and April, UN says
stories.summary: Most of the civilian deaths attributed to drone strikes in the first three months of the year were recorded in the Kordofan region and Darfur, although the strikes were also increasingly spreading beyond both regions.

stories.title: Greek minister says mystery drone from a 'foreign state'
stories.summary: A Greek military source said the robot, found this week off the island of Lefkada, may have been Ukrainian. Kyiv has admitted using drones to target Russian oil tankers in the Mediterranean.

stories.title: Body of missing US soldier recovered off coast of Morocco
stories.summary: 1st Lt Kendrick Lamont Key Jr was participating in military exercise among US, Nato allies and African countries A search team recovered the body of a US soldier who went missing near a cliff during a training exercise in Cap Draa, Morocco, the US army said on Sunday. Moroccan searchers found the remains on Saturday in the water within a mile (1.6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers 'remain asymptomatic' before flights home – video
stories.summary: Spanish health minister Monica Garcia says all those onboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship were 'asymptomatic' after it arrived in Tenerife in the Canary Islands on Sunday. Everyone disembarking will be checked for symptoms, authorities say, and be taken off the ship only once evacuation flights are ready to fly them to their destinations Argentina in spotlight over hantavirus as authorities retrace footsteps of ship’s passengers Dozens of people from cruise ship struck by hantavirus leave T

stories.title: Britain and Ireland’s wildflowers – in pictures
stories.summary: The Eden Project’s National Wildflower Centre is opening entries for its Wildflower Photographer of the Year 2026 competition on 29 May. The contest showcases images of some of Britain and Ireland’s 1,600 wildflower species, and a selection of photos from last year’s competition will be on display at Eden Dock, Canary Wharf, London, during CWG’s Nature Week, from 13 July Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: US and Mexico brace for heatwave as deadly floods hit South Africa
stories.summary: Temperatures soar in California and Arizona, while deluge continues across Western and Northern Cape Heat is expected to intensify across western parts of the US and Mexico this week as a ridge of high pressure pushes temperatures well above the seasonal norm. Daytime highs are forecast to reach 10-15C above average in some areas. The US National Weather Service has issued heat advisories for parts of California and Arizona, with extreme heat warnings in force on Monday and Tuesday in places suc

stories.title: Sharp drop in ‘forever chemicals’ in seabird eggs hailed as win for regulation
stories.summary: Levels of Pfas in northern gannet eggs in Canada fell up to 74% over 55-year period of study Levels of some of the most dangerous Pfas compounds have dramatically fallen in Canadian seabird eggs, which the authors of a new peer-reviewed study say illustrates how regulations are effective. Researchers looked at Pfas levels in the eggs of northern gannets in the St Lawrence Seaway basin over a 55-year period. Pfas levels shot up from the 1960s through the peak of the chemicals’ use in the late 199

stories.title: Philippine presidential hopeful Sara Duterte impeached for second time
stories.summary: Vice-president is accused of misusing public funds and threatening the lives of President Marcos Jr and his wife The Philippine vice-president, Sara Duterte, has been impeached over allegations she misused public funds, amassed unexplained wealth and threatened the lives of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr and his wife, in a case that could complicate her presidential ambitions. Duterte, the daughter of the detained former president Rodrigo Duterte, was impeached by an overwhelming majority of lawm

stories.title: Drug gang attacks ‘force hundreds of Indigenous families to flee’ in Mexico
stories.summary: Violence in Guerrero state has driven as many as 1,000 households from their homes, rights group says Hundreds of Indigenous families have been forced to flee their homes in the mountains of central Mexico by intense attacks from a local criminal group, including drone bombings, an Indigenous rights organisation said on Monday . A gang known as Los Ardillos has been carrying out attacks in Guerrero state for years, but they started to intensify last week. Villages were subjected to eight hours o

stories.title: Why is Hollywood missing from Cannes?
stories.summary: There are no US blockbusters premiering at the Cannes Film Festival this year. So who's snubbing who?

stories.title: Germany's businesses frustrated by economic decline
stories.summary: No one had higher hopes for Friedrich Merz's chancellorship than the German business community. Now, however, early euphoria has given way to bitter disappointment.

stories.title: Somali piracy disrupts global shipping and trade routes
stories.summary: As ships reroute around Africa to avoid Middle East conflict zones, piracy off Somalia is back. The resurgence is driving up insurance, transit times and security costs, putting new pressure on global supply chains.

stories.title: Endemic, epidemic, pandemic: What's the difference?
stories.summary: The Andes hantavirus outbreak shows how easily disease labels are misunderstood — and why they describe patterns of spread, not how dangerous a virus is.

stories.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer 'not walking away' after local election mauling
stories.summary: Keir Starmer has said he takes "responsibility" for Labour's poor election performance, but also for putting matters right again. "I know I have my doubters and I know I need to prove them wrong, and I will," he said.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How it differs from COVID
stories.summary: In 2018-2019, the Andes virus hantavirus spread person-to-person in Argentina. Eleven people died — but it was stopped. Analysis of that outbreak shows the current one can also be stopped.

stories.title: Architecture of remembrance and hope: Daniel Libeskind at 80
stories.summary: Famous for designing the Jewish Museum Berlin and the Ground Zero project in New York, the architect has created dozens of memorable buildings worldwide.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How is the outbreak being contained?
stories.summary: Passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship are returning to their home countries, some with symptoms, many without. What happens to these people and what is being done to contain the spread?

stories.title: Tourist hotspot at 'end of the world' denies causing hantavirus outbreak
stories.summary: The BBC reports from the city of Ushuaia, where experts have been sent to investigate the origins of the outbreak.

stories.title: BBC speaks with civilians inside Iran struggling with impact of war
stories.summary: Iranian authorities have imposed an internet ban but using trusted sources, special correspondent Fergal Keane hears from those traumatised by conflict and government repression.

stories.title: Why Eurovision's fallout over Israel may change the competition forever
stories.summary: With Eurovision facing its biggest boycott in 70 years, questions are growing over the competition's future.

stories.title: Trump to discuss Iran war and trade on first US presidential visit to China since 2017
stories.summary: The US leader was expected to travel to China earlier this year, but his trip was postponed due to the Iran war. It marks the first visit by a US president to China since 2017.

stories.title: Venice Biennale performances confront war and climate fears
stories.summary: Artists at the Venice Biennale used striking performances and immersive installations to explore themes including war, caregiving and human fragility.

stories.title: Eurovision 2026 kicks off with turquoise carpet parade in Vienna
stories.summary: Images show large crowds gather to welcome contestants for the 2026 Eurovision Song Contest on the Turquoise Carpet in Vienna.

stories.title: BBC tests out England and Argentina's World Cup training grounds in Kansas City
stories.summary: Will Grant tested out one of the pitches where England captain Harry Kane and his team will call home in Kansas City.

stories.title: US soldier missing in Morocco found dead in Atlantic Ocean
stories.summary: The disappearances triggered a search-and-rescue operation involving more than 600 personnel from the United States, Morocco and other military partners.

stories.title: Iran executes young aerospace engineer over CIA and Mossad espionage allegations
stories.summary: Iran has executed a 29-year-old aerospace engineer Erfan Shakourzadeh on Monday for alleged spying, despite his claims of torture and coerced confession, according to his note from prison.

stories.title: Vienna unveils giant Eurovision mural
stories.summary: Vienna has unveiled Austria's largest mural on a vacant office tower as part of the Eurovision Song Contest cultural programme.

stories.title: Hundreds of ships mark Hamburg harbour’s 837th anniversary
stories.summary: Hamburg celebrated the 837th anniversary of its harbour with a maritime parade featuring around 250 ships, from historic sailing vessels to modern coast guard boats.

stories.title: Turkish Airlines plane evacuated after landing gear fire in Nepal, officials say
stories.summary: The Himalayan nation is home to some of the world's most remote and tricky runways, flanked by snow-capped peaks and terrain that poses a challenge even for accomplished pilots.

stories.title: Trump's China visit set to test fragile truce
stories.summary: The first US presidential visit to China in almost 10 years will test a fragile tariff truce.

stories.title: Iran warns Europe against sending warships to Hormuz as US talks gap widens
stories.summary: Tehran has warned Europe against the idea of sending warships to the key waterway, as it outlined a conditional proposal on uranium, sanctions relief and a Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.title: Watch: Humanoid robot becomes Buddhist monk in South Korea
stories.summary: A humanoid robot named Gabi became an honorary Buddhist monk during an ordination ceremony held at Seoul’s Jogye Temple on May 6, ahead of Buddha’s birthday celebrations in South Korea.

stories.title: Watch: Soldier on parachuting to remote island to bring Hantavirus aid
stories.summary: A specialist team helped supply critical medical support to Tristan da Cunha in the South Atlantic.

stories.title: 'I will make an order': Macron asks audience to leave if they want to chat during Nairobi summit
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron interrupted a speaker at the Africa Forward summit in Nairobi to call for silence from a noisy audience, telling attendees to leave the room if they wanted to continue chatting during the speeches.

stories.title: Whales could be harmed by diverted ships avoiding Middle East, scientists warn
stories.summary: Ships have rerouted around the Cape of Good Hope since 2023 to avoid conflicts in the Middle East.

stories.title: Man charged with attempting to kill US President Donald Trump pleads not guilty
stories.summary: Besides the attempted-assassination count, Cole Tomas Allen is also charged with assaulting a federal officer with a deadly weapon and two additional firearms counts.

stories.title: 'Total lack of respect': Macron interrupts speaker to ask for silence
stories.summary: The French president stood up during a conference in Kenya to tell the audience to quieten down, saying it was “impossible” for speakers to be heard.
